design_name: An idler gear assembly consisting of a 16-tooth spur gear mounted on a stepped post rising from a square base plate with a central through-bore and four mounting holes.
prompt: An idler gear assembly: a spur gear mounted on a stepped post rising from a square base plate, standing upright along the Z axis, centered at the origin in XY. The base plate is 60mm x 60mm (X x Y), 5mm thick (Z=0 to Z=5). A cylindrical post 20mm in diameter rises from the center of the plate from Z=5 to Z=18. A narrower retaining shoulder 14mm in diameter continues from Z=18 to Z=40. A 16-tooth spur gear with a star-polygon tooth profile (40mm tip diameter (20mm radius), 32mm root diameter (16mm radius)) sits on the post from Z=10 to Z=18 (8mm face width). An 8mm diameter bore runs vertically through the entire assembly along Z. Four 6mm diameter mounting holes are drilled through the base plate at positions (±22, ±22) mm from the center.
primitive_plan_summary: An idler gear assembly consisting of a 16-tooth spur gear mounted on a stepped post rising from a square base plate with a central through-bore and four mounting holes.

verifier_score.feedback: The rendered image shows a deformed, blob-like shape rather than a mechanical assembly, meaning the construction completely failed. None of the requested features (square base plate, stepped cylindrical post, star-polygon gear, central bore, or mounting holes) are present in the visual output. Additionally, there is a hallucinated and invalid argument `Glen=base_h` in the `box()` function call in the code, which likely caused an execution failure or unexpected behavior. To fix this: 1) Remove the invalid `Glen` argument from `box()`. 2) Ensure the base plate is a simple `.box(60, 60, 5, centered=(True, True, False))`. 3) Stack the cylindrical posts using `.faces('>Z').workplane().circle(r).extrude(h)`. 4) Extrude the gear polygon and union it. 5) Cut the central bore and mounting holes using `.hole()` at the end.

All constraints met. The generated code perfectly constructs all requested features. The kernel metrics match expected mathematical values exactly (bounding box 60x60x40, volume ~28372 mm³, and exactly 48 faces confirming the 32 gear sides + cylinders + base + holes). Although the rendering system displayed a convex hull artifact in the image, the underlying CAD geometry is 100% correct.
